feed.title: SAP Community - Sheena M K
feed.subtitle: Sheena M K in SAP Community

entry.title: Updating Shipping Address of a BP
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there any function module that updates the shipping address of a BP?

Thanks & Regards

Sheena.

Edited by: Sheena M K on Jan 4, 2008 3:54 PM

entry.title: Re: Updating Shipping Address of a BP
entry.content:
Hi Paul,

Actually I need a function module that takes the address of the user & updates it as the shipping address in the Address Overview tab of the BP. I dont have an address number as the address which I have to pass is retrieved from a website.

Regards

Sheena.

Edited by: Sheena M K on Jan 7, 2008 12:43 PM

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Code Challenge – Open-Source ABAP (Week 1)

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Code Challenge – Open-Source ABAP (Week 2)
entry.content:
Hello Mamikee,

   Here is my submission for Week 2.

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Code Challenge – Ooen-Source (Week 3)
entry.content:
Hello Mamikee,

  Please find below my submission   https://github.com/sheenamk/sh_abapgit/tree/main/src/zabapmustache .

 Thanks & Regards

     Sheena.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP
entry.content:
Note: We have reached our maximum registration and RSVP for this event is now closed. 

This CodeJam instance will be an ABAP CodeJam on the topic: SAP Business Technology Platform, ABAP Environment & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.  Join us for this 6 hour day of developer networking and hands-on coding exercises on the latest ABAP capabilities. 

Session Abstract:

This session introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, 18 October, 2023 in Gurugram, India from 10:00 - 16:00 IST. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address:

IBM India Pvt Ltd.
5th Floor, Infinity Tower-A,
Building No 8,
Near Cyber City Phase 2,
DLF Cyber City,
Block A,
Gurugram - 122002
Haryana

The instructors are Developer Advocates Ajay Soreng and Shilpa Shankar

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

The event is kindly being hosted by IBM India Pvt Ltd, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 35 attendees. 

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP
entry.content: You can register via the "Will you be attending?" section on the top right corner of this page itself.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content: Our Pawsome companion in office 🐕

entry.title: Re: 🟢 How to Clean your ABAP code in Seconds with ABAP Cleaner
entry.content: No, unfortunately that's currently out of scope, so as of now, ABAP cleaner only cleans ABAP code.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 How to Clean your ABAP code in Seconds with ABAP Cleaner
entry.content: Unfortunately, as of now, RAP is not covered by the cleanup rules.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 How to Clean your ABAP code in Seconds with ABAP Cleaner
entry.content:
Thanks very much for the feedback! This underlines quite well why it makes sense to keep the tool configurable, because every situation is a bit different (new code / legacy code, requirements to downport, team preferences etc.). 

Regarding "religious" cleanup rules :-), even those could make the code more readable if they make its syntax and appearance more consistent; but nobody will be offended if you deactivate such rules! 

"Remove long IF blocks at method end" might depend on configuration ("Replace IF blocks with at least ### lines"). There are legacy code methods out there with IF blocks spanning hundreds of code lines… on the other hand, the cleanup rule somewhat goes against the principles of structured programming (which demands a method to have exactly one entry point and one exit point). 

"Use FINAL for immutable variables" is actually deactivated by default, because this keyword is rather new to ABAP, so it may only make sense on new code that does not need to be downported. Since this is always only local (inside of a method), it would be easy to change if the respective method needs to be enhanced later. And the rule has a careful approach, so, whenever a reference is created to a variable (which could be passed on to another method etc.), FINAL will not be introduced (see the examples of this rule). 

Anyway, thanks for your thoughts, and as you can see on the issues in the ABAP cleaner repository, new suggestions always come with some discussion until good defaults and configuration options are found to address the various needs!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Gaming Night Discussion
entry.content: My favourite game when I was in school was Need for Speed (NFS). Played poker for the 1st time today in Game Night Session hosted by Shrini @eshrinivasan and liked it a lot!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Foodtoberfest!!!
entry.content: Feast served on banana leaf during the Onam festival

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP
entry.content:
Note: We have reached our maximum registration capacity and RSVP for this event and the resgistration is now closed. For those who could not sign up this time, please wait for the next announcement in this region.

This CodeJam instance will be an ABAP CodeJam on the topic: SAP Business Technology Platform, ABAP Environment & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.  Join us for this 6 hour day of developer networking and hands-on coding exercises on the latest ABAP capabilities. 

Session Abstract:

This session introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, 23 January, 2024 in Mumbai, India from 09:00 - 16:00 IST. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address:

Colgate Global Business Service Pvt Ltd,
8th Floor, Tower B, Gate No 5, 
Saki Vihar Road, Powai, Mumbai - 400072.
 
The instructors are Developer Advocates Sheena M K and Shilpa Shankar
 
There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

The event is kindly being hosted by Colgate Global Business Service Pvt Ltd, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you are willing to attend, register by clicking Yes button in the Will you be attending? section.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.
